excluded_defs.name: ExecuteMultiImplAsync
excluded_defs.reason: No longer called; the fix replaces the .Result call with an early throw so the async method is no longer relevant to the sync path

exploration_log.aha_moment: The fix is trivial once the deadlock mechanism is understood: `.Result` on a Task in a single-threaded sync context deadlocks because the continuation needs the same thread. The correct fix is to refuse the combination rather than try to make it work synchronously.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have also looked at ExecuteMultiImplAsync to understand if there's an alternative non-deadlocking synchronous path, but the task explicitly says to throw.

key_decisions.decision: Throw InvalidOperationException directing callers to use ExecuteAsync()
key_decisions.reasoning: The task specification is explicit. Any .Result-based approach can deadlock in single-threaded sync contexts; the correct fix is to prevent the misuse.

solve_notes: Replaced the `.Result` call on `ExecuteMultiImplAsync` (which can deadlock in a single-threaded synchronization context like ASP.NET classic) with an `InvalidOperationException` that directs the caller to use `ExecuteAsync()` instead, as specified by the task.
task_text:
Fix Execute deadlock when CommandFlags.Pipelined is used on sync path

`ExecuteImpl` in `SqlMapper.cs` calls `.Result` on
`ExecuteMultiImplAsync` when `CommandFlags.Pipelined` is set and the
call comes from the synchronous `Execute()` extension method. In
ASP.NET or any context with a single-threaded synchronization context,
this `.Result` call causes a deadlock because the async continuation
cannot resume. Fix `SqlMapper.cs` to detect this unsupported
combination and throw `InvalidOperationException` directing callers to
use `ExecuteAsync()` instead.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ExecuteMultiImplAsync
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would look at this method to understand whether a non-deadlocking synchronous path exists, confirming that throwing is the correct approach

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just `ExecuteImpl` since that is the only edited method. `ExecuteMultiImplAsync` in `SqlMapper.Async.cs` is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent would search for it to understand the async method being called via `.Result`, verify there's no safe sync-over-async alternative, and confirm the throw is the right fix. A skilled human can infer this from the task description alone.
